There's parental guilt, because I just reminded them, you got a $50,000 car in your garage, butt head. You're gonna quibble here over four grand to get the best dental care for your kid, you schmuck. You should be ashamed of yourself for even thinking about price in this, right? I've also gone to there, so I used analogy, right? I did it with the rolls, of course, and now I did it with the other stuff they spend money on, versus the money they spend on their own kid. Then I have split them, right? Our patients tend to be the kind of people. All right? I've made it okay to be an elitist. I've done a lot in a paragraph, right? You might wonder, is everybody a narcissist? So here's a piece of news. This is very kind. This is from some research company in the UK. People who take selfies regularly are much more likely to overestimate how good-looking they are. According to a University of Toronto study. When members of the public rated the selfie takers' photos against the photos of those who don't usually snap pictures of themselves, the regular selfie takers were judged to look less attractive than they thought they would, as well as significantly more narcissistic than the non-selfie takers. Yeah, no shit, right? We had to do, we had to do a study about this, right? All right, we're gonna take a quick break. It's 9.35, so 9.45, 9.50. And we will give away a prize at 9.50.
